prompt: This test always passes even when divide() is broken. What is the bug?
choices: assertEquals must compare two ints, not int and literal 0
The catch block swallows the exception silently — no assertion runs in the catch, so the test passes whether or not an exception is thrown
ArithmeticException is a checked exception and must be declared in the method signature
explanation: 10 / 0 throws ArithmeticException, which skips assertEquals and lands in the catch. The empty catch block does nothing — JUnit sees no failure and marks the test passed. The test gives no signal when the behavior changes. Fix: assertThrows(ArithmeticException.class, () -> 10 / 0); which passes only when the exception is actually thrown.
